title: Projection information design
goal: Turn the shared daemon feed/status projection from a noisy event log into an operator-oriented state summary that answers whether attention is needed, where the run is, and what semantic progress last occurred.
acceptance: The shared feed/status projection is organized into clear altitude tiers: attention first, run state/phase and queue position, last semantic progress, milestones-only activity, and economics/health. Existing CLI monitor/watch/status and Herdr feed panes consume the same wording rather than duplicating prose.

Wrapper-mode runs populate worker_last_semantic_progress_at and a human last-semantic-progress summary from typed Pi tool execution events observed in the wrapper stdout path, without changing worker correctness or result parsing.

Feed activity lines are humanized and bounded: raw JSON object payloads are not printed directly, cockpit-ready and terminal-summary events become human sentences, and consecutive duplicate activity entries are coalesced.

Economics distinguish in-flight work from completed zero work while a worker/gate is active; the display should count started-but-unfinished agent calls or explicitly show in-flight/unknown, never a misleading bare zero during active work.

Golden projection fixtures cover the screenshot warts: no raw JSON in feed lines, no consecutive duplicate activity entries, semantic progress populated from a wrapper-mode tool event fixture, attention block ordering, and in-flight economics wording.

Projection changes are display-only: terminal state, slice state, verification, merge, repair, handoff readiness, evidence attestation, and economics source data remain daemon-owned and unchanged except for the new observability semantic-progress fields.
Terminal notification summaries introduced by HERDR-06 continue to build from the daemon feed/projection and inherit the improved wording without adding a second notification-specific prose path.
must_ask_if: The implementation would make monitor/feed wording depend on Herdr pane text, terminal scrollback, or Pi UI state.
The implementation would parse Pi event names or fields outside src/pi_contract.rs except in tests/fixtures.
The implementation would change run terminal status, verification, merge, repair, handoff, or evidence attestation based on semantic-progress display fields.
The implementation would add a second projection/prose path for Herdr or terminal notifications instead of deepening the shared projection seam.

The implementation would suppress economics entirely for active runs instead of representing in-flight or unknown work truthfully, unless it records a blocker explaining why in-flight counting is not possible in this slice.
